assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows NO (not affected) for all supported versions — every version ships a dependency version outside the CVE's affected range
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a table with 7 versions (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4) all showing 'NO' in the Affected? column. Each ships serde_json >= 1.0.137, above the fixed version 1.0.135. The file states: 'NO supported versions are affected. Every version across all streams ships serde_json >= 1.0.135, which is at or above the fixed version.'

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing as Not a Bug with resolution, not creating remediation tasks (Step 7 Case C)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md heading reads 'Decision: Case C -- No Supported Versions Affected'. Section '2. Transition TC-8002 to Closed' specifies Resolution: 'Not a Bug'. The file explicitly states 'No remediation tasks are required. The recommendation is to close the issue as Not a Bug.' and under 'What Is NOT Being Done': 'No remediation tasks created -- there is nothing to remediate since no versions are affected.'

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting the version impact evidence — listing each version and its dependency version as proof of non-impact
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md section '1. Add triage comment to TC-8002' includes a comment block listing all 7 versions with their serde_json versions: 2.1.0 (1.0.137), 2.1.1 (1.0.137), 2.2.0 (1.0.138), 2.2.1 (1.0.138), 2.2.2 (1.0.138), 2.2.3 (1.0.139), 2.2.4 (1.0.139), all marked NO for Affected?, concluding 'All supported versions ship serde_json >= 1.0.135, which is outside the affected range (versions before 1.0.135). The vulnerable version was never present in any supported release. Closing as Not a Bug (not affected).'

assertion_results.text: VEX Justification is set to 'Component not Present' or appropriate value when the VEX field is configured (Step 7 VEX Justification)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md section '3. Set VEX Justification' specifies: Custom field: customfield_12345 (VEX Justification, configured in Security Configuration), Value: 'Component not Present', with rationale explaining the vulnerable version of serde_json is not present in any supported version. The summary table also confirms: 'VEX Justification | Component not Present (customfield_12345)'.

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT create any remediation tasks — no upstream backport, no downstream propagation (Step 7 Case C closes the issue)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md under 'What Is NOT Being Done' explicitly states: 'No remediation tasks created -- there is nothing to remediate since no versions are affected.' and 'No cross-stream notices -- no versions in any stream are affected, so there is no cross-stream impact to report.' The summary table shows 'Remediation tasks | None'. No remediation task files exist in the outputs directory (only data-extraction.md, triage-outcome.md, version-impact.md).
